story.iWant: a standardized workflow pattern with workflow.yaml and instructions.md files
story.soThat: all commands follow the same BMAD execution model

subtasks.subtask: 1.1: Define workflow.yaml schema (name, description, instructions path, config)
1.2: Implement YAML parsing for workflow configuration
1.3: Resolve variable references ({installed_path}, {project-root})
1.4: Handle missing or malformed workflow.yaml with clear error
2.1: Load instructions.md from path specified in workflow.yaml
2.2: Parse XML step structure (<step n="N" goal="...">)
2.3: Extract action, ask, template-output, and other supported tags
2.4: Handle malformed instructions with clear error
3.1: Execute steps in exact numerical order
3.2: Process <action> tags (perform specified actions)
3.3: Ensure step N completes before step N+1 begins
3.4: Track current step in execution state
4.1: Detect <template-output> tags and pause for user review
4.2: Display generated content to user
4.3: Present approval options ([c] Continue, [y] YOLO, etc.)
4.4: Wait for user response before continuing
4.5: Handle <ask> tags (prompt user and wait for response)
5.1: Create .slide-builder/workflows/test/workflow.yaml with minimal config
5.2: Create .slide-builder/workflows/test/instructions.md with 3 test steps
5.3: Include <template-output> tag to test checkpoints
5.4: Verify end-to-end workflow execution

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given a workflow directory (e.g., .slide-builder/workflows/setup/), when the workflow is invoked, then the system reads workflow.yaml for configuration
The system loads instructions.md for execution logic
The system follows step-by-step instructions with checkpoint approvals
Given the workflow.yaml contains phase definitions, when executing the workflow, then each phase completes before the next begins
User approval is requested at checkpoint tags (<template-output>, <ask>)

doc.title: Slide Builder PRD
doc.snippet: FR48-52 define workflow requirements: Each command maps to workflow definition (workflow.yaml), workflows have instruction files (instructions.md), execute in phases with checkpoint approvals, state in YAML files, extensible for new workflows.

doc.title: Slide Builder Architecture
doc.snippet: Every workflow follows: workflow.yaml (name, description, instructions path) + instructions.md with <workflow><step n="1"><action><template-output> structure. ADR-001 mandates 100% BMAD pattern alignment.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Workflow Executor module: Reads workflow.yaml for config, loads instructions.md, parses XML step structure, executes steps in order with checkpoint pauses, handles template-output tags for file saves.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.snippet: Schema: name (workflow identifier), description (what it does), instructions (path to instructions.md), additional workflow-specific config.

doc.title: Epic 1 Technical Specification
doc.section: Workflows and Sequencing - Workflow Execution Sequence
doc.snippet: 1. User enters slash command 2. Command Router resolves path 3. Load workflow.yaml 4. Load instructions.md 5. For each step: execute actions, handle template-output, process ask tags, update state 6. Report completion.

doc.title: Epic Breakdown
doc.snippet: Establishes standardized workflow pattern. Prerequisites: Story 1.1. Technical Notes: Reference .bmad/core/tasks/workflow.xml for execution model.

doc.title: BMAD Workflow Execution Engine
doc.snippet: Core execution engine with WORKFLOW-RULES: Steps execute in numerical order, template-output tags save+show+approve, supported tags include step, action, ask, template-output, invoke-workflow, goto. This is the authoritative reference for Slide Builder workflow implementation.

file.reason: Primary reference for workflow execution pattern - this is the BMAD workflow engine that Slide Builder must mirror
Target location for workflow definitions created by Story 1.1 - this story will populate with test workflow
Project dependencies (puppeteer, googleapis) - no additional deps needed for Story 1.2

constraints.constraint: 100% BMAD pattern alignment - workflow.yaml + instructions.md structure must mirror .bmad/ exactly
Steps execute in exact numerical order (rule 1)
Template-output tags require: Save content → Show user → Get approval before continuing (rule 3)
Optional steps ask user unless #yolo mode active (rule 2)
State files must be human-readable YAML (NFR17)
No external API calls in Epic 1 - all local file operations
Directory structure exists at .slide-builder/workflows/ - use existing scaffold

interface.name: workflow.yaml schema
interface.signature: name: string # Workflow identifier description: string # What workflow does instructions: string # Path to instructions.md ({installed_path}/instructions.md) config_source: string # Optional external config path variables: object # Optional workflow-specific variables default_output_file: string # Optional output file path template

interface.name: Supported XML Tags
interface.signature: Structural: step n="X" goal="...", optional="true", if="condition", for-each, repeat Execution: action, ask, goto, invoke-workflow, invoke-task, invoke-protocol Output: template-output, critical, example

tests.standards: Testing follows manual verification in Claude Code environment. Per tech-spec: Unit tests verify workflow.yaml loads correctly, Integration tests run test workflow and verify step order, Checkpoint tests verify pause at template-output tags, Edge case tests handle missing directories and malformed YAML. No automated test framework in Epic 1 - all manual verification.

locations.location: Manual CLI testing in Claude Code

ideas.idea: Create test workflow with workflow.yaml, invoke it, verify YAML parsed and name/description accessible
Create instructions.md with 3 steps, verify all steps are parsed and step structure correct
Execute test workflow, verify steps 1,2,3 execute in exact order with console output
Add phase markers to steps, verify step N waits for step N-1 completion
Add template-output tag, verify execution pauses and shows approval prompt ([c] Continue, [y] YOLO)
Invoke non-existent workflow, verify clear error message
Create invalid YAML, verify graceful error handling
Create workflow.yaml pointing to missing instructions.md, verify error
